prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "仰卧臀桥" (supine glute bridge) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the top of a glute bridge: lying supine on the floor with upper back, head, neck, and arms flat on the ground; both feet flat on the ground hip-width apart with both knees bent at 90 degrees; hips lifted upward so that the body forms a straight diagonal line from the knees through the hips to the shoulders; arms resting at the sides with palms down; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: gluteus maximus (the main hip extensors) and hamstring group. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: core muscles (transverse abdominis) and erector spinae. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus; "腘绳肌" pointing to the hamstring group; "核心 / 竖脊肌" pointing to the core and erector spinae area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
